section.intent: Present ticket options or product tiers.
section.contentHints: Three tiers: 'Ghost' (Basic), 'Vampire' (Standard), 'Overlord' (VIP) with price points and list of inclusions.

designPlan.goal: Clearly present options and drive selection of the highest value tier
designPlan.roleFit: Helps the Visitor choose the package that fits their goals
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Pre-form selection step in the conversion loop
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Lead capture by funneling users toward the registration form
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Three-tier comparison table or card set
designPlan.layoutIntent: Horizontal cards with the middle 'Vampire' tier highlighted
designPlan.visualIntent: Distinctive colors for each tier (e.g., Silver, Red, Gold) within the dark theme
designPlan.interactionIntent: CTA buttons on each card that scroll to the form with the tier pre-selected
designPlan.contentStrategy: Use thematic names for tiers to maintain the 'playful horror' mood
constraints: Pricing must be clear and unambiguous
designPlan.rationale: Thematic tier names (Ghost, Vampire, Overlord) make the boring task of price comparison fun and immersive.

pageContext.title: Halloween Promotion Home
pageContext.description: The main landing page designed to immerse users in the Halloween spirit and drive conversions.

pageDesignPlan.pageGoal: The main landing page designed to immerse users in the Halloween spirit and drive conversions.
pageDesignPlan.capabilityFocus: This page should make Thematic Visual Storytelling, Conversion Engine understandable and actionable.
pageDesignPlan.taskLoopCoverage: Help users progress through Event Registration Loop, Event Registration Loop, Event Registration Loop, Event Registration Loop, Event Registration Loop.
pageDesignPlan.narrativeArc: Move from orientation to persuasion, then social proof, and finish with a decisive conversion close.
pageDesignPlan.layoutStrategy: Use section rhythm, spacing, and contrast to support the entry stage of the user journey rather than making every block equally loud.
hierarchy: The first viewport should establish context and value quickly.
Mid-page sections should deepen trust or explain the offer.
Lower-page sections should reduce friction and reinforce action.
pageDesignPlan.transitionStrategy: Make transitions feel intentional through spacing, contrast shifts, and restrained motion rather than decorative separators.
sharedShellNotes: Assume global layout sections frame the page experience.
Keep page-specific sections visually coherent with the shared shell.
constraints: Preserve the given section order unless a wrapper or grouping is required for coherence.
Avoid repetitive section pacing; vary density and emphasis across the page.
pageDesignPlan.rationale: Default page planner framed Halloween Promotion Home around role needs and the MVP journey rather than isolated sections.
